records.ety: [AS. murnan; akin to OS. mornian, OHG. mornen, Goth. marnan.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Mourned (mōrnd); p. pr. & vb. n. Mourning.]

defs: To express or to feel grief or sorrow; to grieve; to be sorrowful; to lament; to be in a state of grief or sadness.
quotes: Abraham came to mourn for Sarah, and to weep for her.

defs: To wear the customary garb of a mourner.
quotes: We mourn in black; why mourn we not in blood?
Grieve for an hour, perhaps, then mourn a year.

defs: To grieve for; to lament; to deplore; to bemoan; to bewail.
quotes: As if he mourned his rival's ill success.
And looking over the hills, I mourn The darling who shall not return.

defs: To utter in a mournful manner or voice.
quotes: The lovelorn nightingale Nightly to thee her sad song mourneth well.
